title: Inquiry-based adaptive prediction
abstract: Predicting future utilization of a resource. The predicting future utilization of a resource may include receiving inquiries for a potential future utilization of the resource for different future points in time, recording time-based patterns of the received inquiries, recording a historic actual utilization value of the resource, and determining a future utilization of the resource using the recorded time-based patterns of the received inquiries, the recorded historic actual utilization value of the resource and a current inquiry pattern of the resource.
claims:
Claims A method (100) for predicting a future utilization of a resource in a future point in time, the method (100) comprising - receiving (102) inquiries (208) of a potential future utilization of the resource for different future points in time, - recording (104) time-based patterns of the received inquiries (208), - recording (106) an actual historic utilization (206) of the resource, and - determining (108) a future utilization of the resource using the recorded time-based patterns of the received inquiries (208), the recorded actual historic utilization values (206) of the resource and a current inquiry pattern for the resource. The method (100) according to claim 1, wherein the actual historic utilization (206) is recorded as an average utilization value during discrete predefined time periods. The method (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the resource is a part of a road- path from a first geo-position to a second geo-position. The method (100) according to claim 3, wherein the inquiring the potential future utilization of the part of the road-path is regularly performed by a navigation system. The method (100) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the determining the future utilization of the resource is also based on a parameter reflecting an external influence factor of the historic actual utilization (206). The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the resource is a trading volume of stocks. The method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the resource is an availability of a time dependent service. 8. A prediction system for a prediction a future utilization of a resource in a future point in time, the prediction system comprising 

 - a receiving unit (302) adapted for receiving inquiries (208) on a potential future utilization of the resource for different future points in time, - a first recording unit (304) adapted for recording time-based patterns of the received inquiries (208), - a second recording unit (306) adapted for recording an actual historic utilization (206) of the resource, and - a determining unit (308) adapted for determining a future utilization of the resource using the recorded time-based patterns of the received inquiries (208), the recorded actual historic utilization values (206) of the resource and a current inquiry pattern for the resource. A computer system (400) comprising the prediction system (204) according to claim 7. A navigation system comprising the prediction system according to claim 7, wherein the resource is a part of a road-path, and wherein the navigation system is adapted for regularly inquiring the potential future utilization of the part of the road-path. A data processing program for predicting a future utilization of a resource in a future point in time according to any of the claims 1 to 7, the data processing program comprising software code portions for performing the method (100), when said data processing program is run on a computer. A computer program product for the method (100) for predicting a future utilization of a resource in a future point in time according to any of the claims 1 to 7, comprising computer readable program means for causing a computer to perform the method (100) when the program means is run on the computer.
